hypothesis: Introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5, while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions. This will increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from other subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong selectivity (Δmargin > 0) but are failing structurally. The next step is to focus on improving structural validity without compromising selectivity by increasing candidate diversity and tightening clash constraints.

proposed_changes.rationale: Low pass rate suggests insufficient diversity in the candidate pool. Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
Structural failures may be due to steric clashes not being adequately penalized. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce invalid structures.
